prompt_replacements.old:
Use NORMAL_BUSINESS_INSTANCE for ordinary business reporting questions over the subject as business users normally understand it.
Use RAW_DATA_RECORD only when the user explicitly asks for persisted records, rows, logs, audit entries, raw data, database entries, or another data artifact.
prompt_replacements.new: The requested result itself determines instance_interpretation. Use RAW_DATA_RECORD when it names a record, row, log, audit entry, database entry, or raw data. Otherwise use NORMAL_BUSINESS_INSTANCE for a business entity or event as normally understood in operations and reporting.

prompt_replacements.old:
After writing answer_population, inspect each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT. When the question supplies a concrete value used by that test, declare the smallest copied value phrase as one predicate_value and reference that input from the test. Do not turn the answer subject or counted unit into an input.
A predicate_value does not identify one particular resource.
prompt_replacements.new:
After writing answer_population, inspect each EXPLICIT_USER_CONSTRAINT. A predicate_value is an explicit word or phrase that states which property value qualifying candidates must have. This includes adjectival and compound modifiers; copy the user's phrase without inferring catalog field boundaries.
Each predicate_value supplies the membership test or tests whose required property value it states. A compound phrase may supply several tests. Do not turn the answer subject or counted unit into an input.
